headline: Large crystal shows quantum entanglement in everyday objects
meta_description: A centimeter-sized crystal displays quantum entanglement, proving large objects can behave in ways once thought impossible.
summary_short: Scientists found clear quantum entanglement in a crystal large enough to see, suggesting stranger physics exists in everyday objects.
body:
For decades, quantum entanglement seemed like a microscopic phenomenon—a quirk of particles far too small to see or touch. But researchers have now observed it clearly in a centimeter-sized crystal, proof that large, ordinary-looking objects can display surprisingly deep quantum behaviour.

This discovery has practical implications. It could help solve puzzles about exotic materials called strange metals. More broadly, it opens doors to ultra-precise quantum sensors and advanced technologies that seemed locked behind the barrier of size. The finding hints that the quantum world is far more woven into everyday reality than scientists once believed.
